code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Product failure within 3 months with safety risks
    - Multiple defects across purchases/updates
    - Explicit "return/don't buy" recommendations
    - Negative updates overriding initial positivity

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failures despite some positives
    - Regret language ("waste of money", "misleading")
    - Comparative inferiority to alternatives
    - Requires constant workarounds for basic use

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - "Does the job but..." phrasing
    - Partial functionality requiring adjustments
    - Target audience restrictions without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific functional issue
    - Maintains recommendation despite clear flaws
    - Resolved issues with lingering mention (e.g. "had smell but...")
    - Short positive statements without enthusiasm
    - Single functional criticism in secondary feature

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic endorsement ("love", "best ever", "exceeded")
    - Multiple complementary aspects praised (3+ features)
    - Long-term validation (6+ months) with sustained performance
    - "Worth every penny" declarations with demonstrated utility
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite noted imperfections
    - Non-functional criticism with core functionality praise

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Safety hazards →1 regardless of positive remarks
    - Multiple purchase failures →1 even if initial experience positive
    - "Perfect except [non-functional quirk]" →5
    - "Would be 5 stars but [functional issue]" →4
    - Temporary issues resolved enthusiastically →5
    - Inherent material properties ≠ product flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (5→4 Error Fix):
Review: "This trash can has silent operation, perfect size, durable build. Minor lid quirk."
<Reasoning>Multiple strengths with trivial non-functional drawback. Comprehensive satisfaction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2 (4→5 Error Fix):
Review: "So far works well. Makes great smoothies with sharp-looking design."
<Reasoning>Functional and aesthetic praise without criticisms. Warrants 5 despite "so far".</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3 (1-Star Critical):
Review: "UPDATE: Failed twice within 6 months - dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multiple safety-related failures override any positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 4 (Borderline 4):
Review: "These decorations are large/visible but lack style versatility."
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy with aesthetic limitation. Lacks enthusiasm.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (5-Star Benchmark):
Review: "Love everything! Silent, space-saving, durable. Best purchase this year!"
<Reasoning>Enthusiastic endorsement with multiple features praised.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6 (2-Star Regret):
Review: "Beautiful but cracks under normal use. Wouldn't recommend."
<Reasoning>Core failure despite aesthetic appeal.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7 (3-Star Compromise):
Review: "Gets job done with frequent adjustments. Okay for price."
<Reasoning>Adequate performance with usability compromises.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8 (4-Star Imperfect):
Review: "EDIT: After washing, towels work well but initial absorbency poor."
<Reasoning>Resolved issue still mentioned as functional drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9 (5-Star Update):
Review: "A year later - still perfect! Minor noise irrelevant."
<Reasoning>Long-term validation outweighs trivial drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10 (5-Star Material):
Review: "Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than competitors. Worth it!"
<Reasoning>Material property outweighed by core functionality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (34 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 3 -> 2: 2

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "Fit perfect into my CHV1210."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "does exactly what I need it to do. Not too thick and fits well. has deep pockets to fit about any thickness of mattress"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [4, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "These mats are fine if you like to switch them out a lot. If you refer to have a very fresh and new bathmat for the bathroom when guests are staying for a visit, then these have a fun knobbly like texture to them and are cheap enough to pick up for s..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 34 errors, 22 borderline out of 80 examples."}
